narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know hearing that your law school application was rejected because our model predicts a 52.9% chance of not passing the bar exam feels like a gut punch, especially when you've worked so hard to get here. Picture the model looking at your story like a coach scouting a promising athlete—it weighs the early signs of your academic journey most heavily, and yours started with a Year 1 decile of 3.0, which stood out as the biggest flag pushing toward this tougher outlook. Your LSAT score of 35.0 built on that concern, signaling to the model a foundation that might struggle with the bar's intense demands. Then came your Year 3 decile of 2.0, reinforcing the sense that grades haven't climbed as steadily as they do for many who succeed. Even with your solid undergraduate GPA of 3.3 and upper-middle family income, these pulled in the same direction, suggesting resources alone might not bridge the gap the model sees. Your background as a white female pursuing full-time studies layered on subtly, fitting patterns where similar profiles have faced steeper odds. It's not that any one piece defines you—it's this combination painting a picture of early hurdles that, in the model's view, add up to higher risk. This isn't a judgment on your potential or heart; it's a data-driven snapshot meant to guide tough choices. Many in your shoes have defied similar predictions by targeting those early weaknesses head-on. If you boost your academic deciles and LSAT prep, you could rewrite this story entirely. You've got the drive—now channel it where it counts most. We're rooting for your next chapter.
